architecture_fit.note: Real multi-head attention forward (embed -> Q/K -> scaled dot-product+softmax) with a single ALiBi-style relative-position-bias delta, no MLP; directly produces the distance decay and per-head local/global structure the goal asks about.

baseline_comparison.note: Flat uniform baseline (slope 0) plus a bias-ablated control both sit flat while the model decays (slope 0.53) — strawman failure is explicit and quantitative.

faithfulness.note: Identical forward with the bias term zeroed collapses the headline slope 0.53 -> 0.00 onto the uniform baseline, a clean causal ablation isolating the relative-position-bias circuit.

operating_range.note: Decay holds smoothly across distances 0.5–48 (~2 orders of magnitude) and per-head lambda spans 1.5–64, but no explicit sweep over seq length / input scale or breakdown point is shown.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Fully hand-set deterministic weights (embeddings, Q/K projections, per-head lambda slopes) reproduce the target distance decay — strong evidence of mechanism understanding.

visual_judgement.note: Demo overlays model vs uniform vs ablated on one log-log decay axis plus a per-(layer,head) slope heatmap; the claim and its causal control are legible at a glance.

visualisation_rationale.note: README justifies both plots and ties them to the rubric: headline + strawman + causal control in plot 1, local→global head gradient matching the layer_head metrics in plot 2.

notes: A genuinely faithful hand-built attempt: distance decay is emitted by a real attention softmax over QK+ALiBi-bias logits (single delta from base_model.py, no MLP), and a clean bias-ablation collapses the headline slope 0.53 -> 0.00 onto the uniform baseline as causal evidence. Baselines, per-head local/global gradient, and a two-panel demo all directly serve the goal. Only weak point is operating range — decay is shown across the distance axis but there is no explicit sweep over input/sequence scale; mean of scored items lands in the 'good' band.
